plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and stability.
Sell QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to exit flagged holdings and remove all leveraged ETFs.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name exposure.
This plan aims for capital preservation and a large cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces overall risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but still carries market exposure.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to exit all flagged holdings and remove leveraged ETFs.
Increase CRWD to 18% to lean into a strong performer.
Buy MSFU and OXY at 10% each, adding top scanner setups.
This plan reduces leverage and diversifies into new quality names.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality setups. Expect moderate drawdowns in market corrections.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, SMH, and SPAL to exit flagged holdings and underperforming leveraged ETF.
Increase CRWD to 20% to lean into a strong performer.
Add SOXL back to 10% for leveraged semiconductor exposure.
Buy MSFU at 15% as a top scanner setup.
Watch SOXS for a potential inverse semiconductor hedge if market sentiment shifts.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month but comes with significantly higher risk. Leverage amplifies both gains and losses, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay. Expect larger drawdowns and strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration.
Increase QQQI to 40% for a strong income focus.
Sell QQQ, CRWD, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to remove flagged holdings, high-beta names, and all leveraged ETFs.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady distributions and capital preservation. While aiming for consistent income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs. This is not a savings account.
